header.name: Dr. Timothy J. Owen
header.subtitle: U.S. Army Veteran · IT Professional · South Puget Sound, WA

objective: U.S. Army veteran with 20 years of leadership and an active federal Secret-level security clearance, transitioning from distinguished military service to Information Technology. Holding **CompTIA Network+**, **AWS Cloud Practitioner**, **Foundational C#**, and **Certified Entry-Level Python Programmer** certifications, and two completed Washington Vets-to-Tech programs (Server & Cloud Administration, Aug 2025; Cybersecurity, Dec 2025), I am eager to deliver secure, compliant IT support in dynamic, mission-critical environments—ensuring seamless operations and exceptional service.

transferableSkills.title: Analytical Thinking
transferableSkills.description: Designed SRS systems and AMPA rubrics — directly applicable to IT process optimization and troubleshooting workflows.

transferableSkills.title: Automation
transferableSkills.description: Built Python tools (Subnet Calculator, VA Ratings Calculator, Tempo Finder) using algorithmic problem-solving relevant to IT scripting and infrastructure support.

transferableSkills.title: Adaptability
transferableSkills.description: Sustained mission-critical operations across 20 years of military service and rapid IT upskilling — translatable to dynamic, fast-moving technical environments.

transferableSkills.title: Organization
transferableSkills.description: Managed HR, security clearances, library operations, and $300K+ equipment inventory — directly applicable to IT asset and data management.

projects.title: Subnet Calculator
projects.description: Python tool automating IPv4 subnet calculations for network troubleshooting. Demonstrates scripting, documentation, and process optimization.

projects.title: VA Ratings Calculator
projects.description: Python tool automating VA disability ratings calculations, demonstrating scripting, data processing, and veteran-focused problem solving.

projects.title: Tempo Finder
projects.description: Binary-search algorithm identifying optimal practice tempos (30–240 BPM) at O(log n) efficiency. Showcases algorithmic problem-solving and user-focused design.

projects.title: Music Practice SRS
projects.description: Anki-based spaced repetition system for saxophone practice and study preparation. Applied SuperMemo principles to automated scale/pattern generation.
